title: lepidium pratense subsp. reverchonii
intro: Lepidium pratense subsp. reverchonii, also known as French cress or poorman's pepper, is a subspecies of the herbaceous plant Lepidium pratense. It is a type of mustard plant native to Europe and North Africa, and is characterized by its small, white or pale pink flowers and peppery-tasting leaves. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is said to have various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure and improving digestion. Lepidium pratense subsp. reverchonii can be consumed as a tea, added to salads, or used as a spice, and is also used in traditional herbal preparations such as infusions and tinctures.
